rulings.Case Title: NORMAN SIERRA VS. AMBASSADOR PRIVATE SECURITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 15, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE'S MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 583.210 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DID NOT EFFECTUATE SERVICE WITHIN THREE YEARS OF FILING OF THE COMPLAINT, AND THEREFORE DISMISSAL IS MANDATORY UNDER THE STATUTE. SERVICE ON A GENERAL MANAGER OF A STORE OPERATED BY THE CORPORATION IS INSUFFICIENT TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP SECTION 416.10. (SEE BAKERSFIELD HACIENDA V. SUPERIOR COURT (1962) 199 CAL.APP.2D 798, 803, "THE GENERAL MANAGER OF A MOTEL IS NOT THE GENERAL MANAGER OF THE CORPORATION...THE INTENTION OF THE LEGISLATURE...IS TO PERMIT SERVICE TO BE MADE UPON THE GENERAL MANAGER OF THE CORPORATION AND NOT SIMPLY ON A MANAGING AGENT OF ONE PART OF ITS BUSINESS." PLAINITFF ALSO DID NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP SECTION 415.20 BECAUSE NO EVIDENCE WAS PRESENTED THAT PLAINTIFF MAILED A COPY OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT TO THE PLACE OF BUSINESS, AS REQUIRED BY THE STATUTE. DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS UNDER CCP SECTION 128.5(A) IS DENIED. DEFENDANT DID NOT PRESENT SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF ENGAGED IN 'BAD-FAITH ACTIONS OR TACTICS THAT ARE FRIVOLOUS OR SOLELY INTENDED TO CAUSE UNNECESSARY DELAY." ANY PARTY WHO CONTESTS A TENTATIVE RULING MUST SEND AN EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO ALL OTHER PARTIES BY 4PM STATING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, THE PORTION(S) OF THE TENTATIVE RULING THAT THE PARTY CONTESTS. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WITH THE NAME AND ADDRESS OF THE PREVAILING PARTY'S COUNSEL OR THE PREVAILING PARTY IF PRO PER IN THE TOP LEFT OF THE FIRST PAGE OF PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE CODE OF CIVIL RPOCEDURE 437C(g) AND MUST BRING THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE HEARING OR EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG PRIOR TO THE HEARING EVEN IF THE MOTION IS NOT OPPOSED OR THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTESTED. =(302/LCN)

rulings.Case Title: OLLIE ROBINSON JR VS. NODDIE BELL ROBINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Marilyn Chism'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 15, 2013, Line 7: Defendant MARILYN CHISM'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Quash Deposition Subpoena is GRANTED, without prejudice to other methods of discovery of same subject matter directed to the parties, and to another subsequent subpoena for deposition of same third party, more narrowly drawn, after other methods of discovery have proven to be unsuccessful or inadequate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERICA SKONE-REES VS. WEST ELM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 15, 2013, Line 3: Defendants WILLIAMS-SONOMA, INC. and WEST ELM DBA WILLIAMS-SONOMA STORES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: With respect to the Special Interrogatories, the motion is denied. Special Interrogatories, Set 3 violate C.C.P. §2030.030(a)(1) and §2030.050. With respect to the Request for Admissions, plaintiff/responding party failed to object to the number of Request for Admissions. Plaintiff is required to provide further responses to Request for Admissions 45-57 without objection, except plaintiff may "refuse to disclose any matter that may tend to incriminate her" pursuant to the 5th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution and Art. I, § 15 of the California Constitution. (Evid. Code §940.) Admissibility of such response at trial is not addressed in this decision. Plaintiff's objections to Request for Admissions 58 & 59 are overruled, and plaintiff is required to provide responses to Request for Admissions 58 & 59 without objection, but in conformance with the requirements of C.C.P. §2033.220. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR N. FUENTES VS. WHOLE FOODS MARKET et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories & Request For Production Of Documents; To Compel Attendance Of Party Affiliated Witness At Deposition; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 15, 2013, Line 6: Plaintiff OSCAR FUENTES' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories & Request For Production Of Documents; To Compel Attendance Of Party Affiliated Witness At Deposition; And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David May, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents is denied. Plaintiff waived right to compel further responses. C.C.P.§2030.300(c) & §2031.310(c). Denial is without prejudice to motion to compel further answers following supplemental answers provided on Sept. 30, 2013, if it is otherwise allowed by law. Motion to compel deposition denied as moot. Denial is without prejudice to renewed motion to compel deposition of Charlie Cardinale if he is not produced for deposition on December 5, 2013 (or some other mutually agreeable date). No sanctions awarded to either party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidedwardmay@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA PACE VS. TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for Protective Order Precluding Further Depositions is GRANTED in part. Depositions noticed for a date after October 4, 2013 shall not proceed. Any depositions noticed for October 3 or October 4, 2013 which were not completed due to the motion for protective order shall be rescheduled and completed prior to Arbitration. Moving Party's written objection pursuant to C.C.P. §2025.410 was served on Oct. 1, 2013, at the earliest, and could not apply to depositions noticed for dates within 3 days of that objection. rulings.Case Title: MARK HARPER VS. SEYFARTH SHAW LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 15, 2013. Line 20 - DEFENDANT SEYFARTH SHAW LLP's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant's special motion to strike Plaintiff's complaint for legal malpractice under CCP 425.16 is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant has made a threshold showing that the alleged conduct arises from protected activity under the anti-SLAPP statute. The alleged conduct is written and oral communication by Seyfarth Shaw Attorney, Mr. Newland, with plaintiff's former attorney, Mr. Epstein, regarding a "fraudulent settlement" in connection with the Charles Schwab litigation that commenced in 2008. This oral and written communication is in direct connection with the Charles Schwab litigation, and is protected activity under CCP 425016(e)(2) and the litigation privilege under Civil Code 47. Consequently,the burden shifts to Plaintiff to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on his claim. Plaintiff has not met his burden as he has failed to produce evidence that an attorney-client relationship existed with Mr. Newland and Defendant Seyfarth Shaw, or the Seyfarth Shaw owed a duty to Plaintiff. Defendant's request for prevailing party attorney fees under CCP 425.16(c) is denied without prejudice. Defendant has not filed a declaration of evidence to support the award of attorney fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevaling party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if Pro Per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/LCN)
